pi_items.source: setup_task.sh creates this file at this exact path
setup_task.sh prepares this directory, task description specifies this save location
setup_task.sh prepares this directory, task description specifies this export location

Incident log contains 8 distinct timestamped events: spam attack [18:00], lag [21:00], pool exhaustion [22:00], wipe attempt [23:00], rm -rf [23:27], data loss detected [23:28], restoration starts [00:00], service restored [18:14]
Appears in incident log line: '[23:27] CRITICAL: Engineer accidentally executes "rm -rf" on the PRIMARY database directory'
Word 'delete' (lowercase) does NOT appear in incident log. Log contains 'Deletion' (capitalized) in '[23:28] Deletion detected and process stopped' but not the claimed keyword 'delete'
Appears in incident log line: '[23:28] Deletion detected and process stopped. Approximately 300GB of production data lost.'
Appears in incident log line: '[23:27] CRITICAL: Engineer accidentally executes "rm -rf" on the PRIMARY database directory (meant for secondary).'
Appears in incident log line: '[18:14] (Feb 01) Service fully restored and public access enabled.'
Appears in incident log line: '[18:14] (Feb 01) Service fully restored and public access enabled.'
Appears in incident log line: '[18:14] (Feb 01) Service fully restored and public access enabled.' This matches official GitLab timeline: Service restored at 6:14 PM UTC on Feb 1 = 18:14 UTC
All verified as valid hex color codes with red hue values (0-30° or 330-360°). #f8cecc, #ff0000, #e06666 are standard red shades; #b85450 is dark red
All verified as valid hex color codes with green hue values (100-160°). #d5e8d4, #00ff00, #93c47d are standard green shades; #82b366 is sage/olive-green
Official GitLab post-mortem at https://about.gitlab.com/blog/gitlab-dot-com-database-incident/. Web search confirmed this was a major real incident
Incident log and official GitLab timeline both state rm -rf was executed at 11:27 PM UTC on Jan 31
Official GitLab post-mortem states 295.5 GB of data deleted out of ~300GB total, leaving 4.5GB. Incident log approximates this as '300GB' which is accurate
Both incident log and official GitLab post-mortem confirm service was fully restored and public access enabled at 18:14 UTC on Feb 1

privileged_info_summary: The task uses data from the real 2017 GitLab database deletion incident (January 31 - February 1, 2017). The incident log accurately represents key event times: spam attack initiated at 18:00 UTC, replication lag at 21:00 UTC, database pool exhaustion at 22:00 UTC, and the critical rm -rf command executed at 23:27 UTC on January 31, resulting in deletion of approximately 300GB of production data. Service was fully restored at 18:14 UTC on February 1 using a 6-hour-old snapshot. The incident log contains 8 distinct events (exceeding the minimum of 6), with appropriate keywords for critical events (rm -rf, 300gb, accident) and recovery events (restored, public access, 18:14). All color codes are valid hex values representing appropriate shades of red and green. One metadata claim is contradicted: the keyword 'delete' does not appear in the incident log (only 'Deletion' with capital D), though other keywords sufficiently identify the critical event.
